summary_one_line: Input needs a valid tRNS chunk so num_trans!=0 and PNG_EXPAND_tRNS is set for a non-palette color type.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed PNG whose IHDR declares a non-palette color type (so the else branch at 4790 is taken) plus a structurally valid tRNS chunk that makes png_ptr->num_trans != 0 AND sets the PNG_EXPAND_tRNS transformation. This depends on chunk type bytes and length fields being correct enough for libpng to parse the chunk and accept the transparency, not just a single magic literal.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog winner seeds keep the chunk/header byte region intact and consistent (offsets 0x35-0x3d are uniformly 73 52 47 42 ... 'sRGB' across all 10 W seeds, and IHDR color-type byte 0x19 is uniformly 02 = RGB, a non-palette type), preserving a parseable chunk layout that lets the tRNS chunk register so num_trans!=0 and PNG_EXPAND_tRNS fires; W is the only role that ever takes the true side of the PNG_EXPAND_tRNS operand at L4793.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds corrupt the IHDR/chunk bytes (offset 0x19 holds 00 in 8 of 10 L seeds rather than the uniform 02 of W, and the chunk-name region 0x35-0x3d carries stray non-'sRGB' bytes like 74/45/58 'tEXt'-style), so the tRNS condition never holds: every L seed takes the false branch at L4792 and L4793 is never reached as true.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand into the input wherever the other operand's bytes appear, repairing the chunk-name / header comparands that libpng tests while parsing chunks; naive can only stumble on these multi-byte structural constraints by chance havoc, which never lands in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the tRNS expansion guard requiring num_trans!=0 and PNG_EXPAND_tRNS in the non-palette else arm.
The second operand of the gate is the PNG_EXPAND_tRNS transformation flag.
Only the winner role ever takes the true side of the PNG_EXPAND_tRNS operand at L4793; the loser never reaches it true.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4793  T=181 F=0  T=0 F=0  (png_ptr->transformations & PNG_EXPAND_tRNS) != 0)

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds hold a uniform intact chunk-name region (sRGB) at offsets 0x35-0x3d while loser seeds carry stray non-sRGB bytes there.
Winner seeds have a uniform non-palette color-type byte 02 at offset 0x19 while loser seeds mostly hold 00 there.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator and relies solely on havoc/token byte mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3933 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 230924cbdaa2cc5c` and observed an I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 of the 4-level ancestor chain; the tool reported '1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 3', a lower-bound I2S signal exclusive to cmplog/vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a single fixed multi-byte tRNS magic literal where naive resolves the branch about as often as cmplog within the same budget, showing the structural chunk constraint is not what I2S substitution is solving.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff shows multi-byte structural divergence (color-type and chunk-name regions) rather than one clean concentrated comparand, so the exact CMP operand I2S substitutes to satisfy num_trans!=0/PNG_EXPAND_tRNS is inferred from chunk-parsing semantics, not pinned to a specific offset.
